tests_added.name: test_full_corpus_of_special_chars_roundtrip
tests_added.edge_case: Single record exercises all 5 corruption vectors simultaneously: tashkeel + ZWNJ + tatweel + superscript alef + presentation forms

learnings: Pydantic v2 model_dump(mode='json') does NOT apply Unicode normalization — strings pass through as-is. No NFC/NFD/NFKC/NFKD transformation occurs.
json.dumps(ensure_ascii=False) preserves all Arabic characters including presentation forms (U+FB50–U+FDFF), superscript alef (U+0670), ZWNJ (U+200C), and tatweel (U+0640) without escaping.
json.loads also preserves these characters when reading back — the full write→disk→read cycle is byte-for-byte safe for all tested Arabic Unicode.
The writer implementation is correct and does not corrupt Arabic text. The 35 new tests constitute a permanent regression guard for this guarantee.
Presentation form chars (U+FDFA ﷺ, U+FDFD ﷽, U+FB50) are NOT normalized to base chars by either Pydantic or Python's json module — they are preserved as written.
All 8 diacritic forms (fathah, dammah, kasrah, sukun, shadda, tanwin fath/damm/kasr) and stacked diacritics survive the full serialization cycle.
